mechanism: Implicit learning of word-response contingencies reduces response threshold for predicted response. Item-specific frequency information creates associative predictions that modulate response preparation speed without requiring strategic attention control.

Word primes activate general action or inaction concepts. Activation spreads to semantically related motor or cognitive output channels. Goal satisfaction decreases prime effects via outcome-monitoring mechanism.

Moral agency and patiency map to orthogonal dimensions of mind perception. Perceiver infers complementary role in dyad: agent casts partner as patient. Inverse relation holds across manipulated agency or patiency.

Binary categorical perception of political identity yields different weightings over five moral foundation dimensions. Liberals emphasize individualizing foundations; conservatives weight all five equally. Text analysis and implicit associations confirm explicit moral judgments.

Emotional response (disgust sensitivity) biases moral intuition independent of explicit reasoning. Implicit measure reveals disapproval that explicit judgment suppresses. Dual-process architecture: intuitive judgment uncorrelated with deliberate moral stance.

Visual perspective (first vs third person) bidirectionally links to action abstraction level. Third-person imagery activates abstract representation (why/goals). Abstract representation primes third-person perspective. Mental imagery codes conceptual level via viewpoint.
